content:
- Final required gates all passed for the operator CLI rename task: make docs-build, make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.
- Marked the task file completed with passes=true and checked acceptance criteria.
- Ran /bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh, which updated current_tasks/current_tasks_done/task_history and advanced Ralph off this task.
- Next step is only repository closeout: stage all modified and new files, commit with the required task-finished message including gate evidence, push, and stop.

- Repository closeout completed cleanly.
- Committed the task as 39b90cf with the required `task finished ...` message, including gate evidence and the docs-reference correction note.
- Post-commit hook rebuilt/published the mdBook successfully and pushed the synced docs repo commit 916018b2.
- git push succeeded for the main repo as well.
- Worktree is clean; stopping now.

- Read `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md` for active and completed work.
- Confirmed there are no active unblocked failing bug tasks; active failing work is five medium-priority ctl/operator tasks and four low-priority HA test tasks.
- Checked all active candidate files for `<blocked_by>` tags and found none. Chose `.ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/02-task-add-config-backed-ctl-contexts-and-auto-auth.md` because config-backed `pgtm` context/auth/TLS discovery is the clearest foundation for the remaining operator-experience tasks.

- Created `.ralph/current_task.txt` because it was missing in the workspace.
- Wrote only the selected task path: `.ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/02-task-add-config-backed-ctl-contexts-and-auto-auth.md`.
- Verified the file content and stopped immediately after task selection, per the Choose-Task instructions.

- Started new turn by reading prior Ralph progress and .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Active task is .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/02-task-add-config-backed-ctl-contexts-and-auto-auth.md.
- Next I am reading the task file to determine whether I must write the initial plan, skeptically revise TO BE VERIFIED, or execute NOW EXECUTE.

- Read the active task file header and acceptance criteria.
- It appears to still be pre-execution, so I am checking whether a prior engineer already appended a plan/verification marker lower in the file.
- If no plan exists, this turn will only write the full plan plus TO BE VERIFIED and then stop, per task instructions.

- Confirmed the file has no existing plan markers.
- I am now mapping the code surfaces that the plan must touch: config schema/loading, the pgtm CLI path, integration tests, and operator docs.
- Goal is to write an execution plan that is specific enough to be directly followed on the later NOW EXECUTE pass.

- Read the main CLI/config/test/docs surfaces.
- I noticed the acceptance text mentions future commands like primary/replicas/watch while the current code only has status/switchover, so I am checking sibling story tasks to keep the execution plan scoped correctly.
- The plan needs to preserve room for those later tasks without overloading this one.

- Finished the required planning-only pass for the active config-backed pgtm task.
- Appended a detailed execution plan to the task file, including schema/parser changes, shared secret/env resolution, CLI context architecture, tests, docs, and the verification sequence.
- Ended the plan with TO BE VERIFIED and intentionally stopped without implementation, because the task workflow requires quitting immediately after the first full plan is written.

- Started by reading the Ralph progress log and the active task pointer.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-ctl-operator-experience/02-task-add-config-backed-ctl-contexts-and-auto-auth.md and that the worktree already contains prior task/planning changes.
- I am reading the task file now to determine whether this turn must perform the skeptical plan review or execute an approved plan.

- Read the active task file and found it in TO BE VERIFIED state.
- Performed a skeptical review against the actual code surfaces and altered the plan in two concrete ways: narrowed the shared resolver refactor to the real duplicated loaders and split workflow testing between tests/cli_binary.rs and the HA harness.
- Replaced the task marker with NOW EXECUTE and am starting implementation from the config/schema layer.

- Implemented the core config/schema changes: added optional `[pgtm]` config, env-backed `SecretSource`, and shared config materialization helpers.
- Wired `pgtm` through `-c/--config` with resolved API URL, auth, and TLS context, while keeping direct flags as explicit overrides.
- Updated server-side token resolution, added config-backed CLI smoke coverage plus HA fixture usage, and rewrote the main operator docs/examples around `pgtm -c`.
- Next step is gate-driven debugging: docs build, make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.
